proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_042=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_042=0.
Patients with feature_056=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_056=0.
Patients with feature_111=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_111=0.
Patients with feature_015=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_015=0.
Patients with feature_048=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_048=0.
Patients with feature_040=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_040=0.
Patients with feature_039=1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_039=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.408 (feature_042=1, n=17428) vs 4.310 (feature_042=0, n=32572); diff=+1.098 mo.
Mean pfs_months 3.617 (feature_056=1, n=14969) vs 5.153 (feature_056=0, n=35031); diff=-1.536 mo.
Mean pfs_months 4.865 (feature_111=1, n=34886) vs 4.297 (feature_111=0, n=15114); diff=+0.568 mo.
Mean pfs_months 4.328 (feature_015=1, n=17365) vs 4.887 (feature_015=0, n=32635); diff=-0.559 mo.
Mean pfs_months 3.750 (feature_048=1, n=5090) vs 4.800 (feature_048=0, n=44910); diff=-1.050 mo.
Mean pfs_months 4.317 (feature_040=1) vs 4.776 (feature_040=0); diff=-0.459 mo.
Mean pfs_months 4.824 (feature_039=1) vs 4.462 (feature_039=0); diff=+0.362 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_067 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_101 (continuous, range 1-100) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_019 (39-level continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_063 (3-level categorical) shows a monotonic decrease in mean pfs_months from level 0 to level 2.
feature_011 (race/ethnicity: white/hispanic/black/asian/other) is associated with mean pfs_months.
feature_089 (insurance: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) is associated with mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015 between feature_080 and pfs_months — strongest single association in cohort.
Pearson r=-0.1155 between feature_067 and pfs_months.
Pearson r=-0.0914 between feature_101 and pfs_months.
Pearson r=+0.1002 between feature_019 and pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months: level0=5.640 (n=17592), level1=4.444 (n=24971), level2=3.289 (n=7437). Monotonic decline; level2 vs level0 coefficient = -2.351 mo.
Mean pfs_months by race: white=4.679, hispanic=4.707, black=4.717, asian=4.735, other=4.762. ANOVA F-test p=0.494 — no detectable association.
Mean pfs_months by insurance: private=4.685, medicare=4.692, medicaid=4.706, uninsured=4.733. ANOVA F-test p=0.818 — no detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model adjusting for the leading univariate hits (feature_080, feature_063, feature_042, feature_056, feature_111, feature_015, feature_048, feature_040, feature_039, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101), feature_042 retains a large positive independent association with pfs_months and feature_056 retains a large negative association.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_042 + feature_056 + feature_111 + feature_015 + feature_048 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_034', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model R^2=0.792. Independent effects: feature_080 +0.176/unit, feature_063 level2 -2.335 vs level0, feature_042 +1.102, feature_056 -1.549, feature_111 +0.531, feature_015 -0.582, feature_048 -0.984, feature_040 -0.471, feature_067 -0.077/unit, feature_019 +0.476/unit, feature_101 -0.019/unit. feature_039 (+0.014, p=0.32) and feature_034 (+0.002, p=0.87) attenuated to null after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_042 on pfs_months differs in magnitude across feature_063 levels (treatment effect modified by stage-like variable).
The positive effect of feature_111 on pfs_months differs in magnitude across feature_063 levels.

analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect ≈ +1.10 mo within each feature_063 level: level0 +1.115, level1 +1.093, level2 +1.149. Interaction terms p=0.66 and p=0.60 — no effect modification by feature_063. feature_042 acts additively across stage.
feature_111 effect roughly constant across feature_063 levels (~+0.54 mo). Interaction p-values 0.405 and 0.925.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 and feature_111 show a positive synergistic interaction on pfs_months: the combined-positive group has a mean pfs_months larger than the sum of the individual main effects.
feature_042 and feature_056 show an interaction on pfs_months (effect of feature_042 differs by feature_056 status).
feature_042 and feature_048 show an interaction on pfs_months.
feature_042 and feature_015 show a negative interaction: feature_015=1 abolishes the feature_042 benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: 042=0/111=0 4.30; 042=0/111=1 4.31; 042=1/111=0 4.29; 042=1/111=1 5.89. Both main effects null when feature_111 is absent (p=0.67 for 042, p=0.71 for 111); the interaction term is +1.594 mo (p<1e-300). feature_042 only confers benefit in feature_111-positive patients.
feature_056 has a large independent negative effect (-1.54), feature_042 +1.10. Interaction term +0.006, p=0.90 — additive, no effect modification.
Both main effects sizable (042 +1.10, 048 -1.03). Interaction -0.041, p=0.59 — additive.

Cell means: 042=0/015=0 4.31; 042=0/015=1 4.32; 042=1/015=0 5.97; 042=1/015=1 4.35. Interaction term -1.623 mo (p<1e-200). feature_015=1 essentially abolishes the feature_042 benefit. feature_015 is a negative predictor of feature_042 response, not a prognostic factor in untreated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_042 on pfs_months is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously feature_111-positive and feature_015-negative; in all other strata the effect is null. (Three-way interaction.)
feature_111 and feature_015 also interact: feature_111 main effect is positive only when feature_015=0.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way coefficient = -2.428 mo (p<1e-100). Cell means show 042=1/111=1/015=0 group has pfs_months 6.680 (n=8034), all other 7 cells cluster near 4.3 mo. Two-way 042×111 effect within 015=0 = +2.42 mo; within 015=1 it shrinks to ≈0. The 042 benefit (~+2.4 mo) is confined to 111+/015− patients (≈45% of cohort).

feature_111 main effect = +0.845 mo when feature_015=0; interaction term -0.800 mo (p=4e-57) collapses the benefit when feature_015=1. This is a downstream signature of the underlying 042×111×015 structure (patients with feature_042=1 dominate the mean shift).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (likely age, range 30-90) has a primarily linear positive relationship with pfs_months; the curvature term contributes little incremental fit.
The treatment-like effect of feature_042 is roughly homogeneous across tertiles of feature_080 (no strong age × treatment interaction).
feature_080 effect on pfs_months is attenuated at higher feature_063 levels (i.e., the per-unit feature_080 slope decreases with increasing feature_063).

analyses.result_summary: Linear-only R^2=0.4922; quadratic R^2=0.4932 (Δ=0.001). Quadratic coefficient +0.0006/unit^2 (p<1e-24) is technically significant but explains negligible additional variance. Mean pfs_months by quartile is essentially linear: Q1 2.50, Q2 4.09, Q3 5.27, Q4 6.96.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_080', df).fit() + tertile stratification
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect by feature_080 tertile: young +1.139, mid +1.053, old +1.099. Interaction coefficient -0.0032/unit (p=0.044, marginal). Practically homogeneous treatment effect across age.

feature_080 slope by feature_063 level: level0 +0.1790/unit, level1 +0.1761, level2 +0.1661. Interaction with level2 = -0.0129 (p=4e-9) — feature_080 confers slightly less per-unit benefit at the highest feature_063 level, but the qualitative direction is preserved.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race/ethnicity (feature_011) modifies the magnitude of the feature_042 treatment effect on pfs_months.
Insurance type (feature_089) modifies the magnitude of the feature_042 treatment effect on pfs_months.
In the predicted-responder subgroup (feature_111=1, feature_015=0), the feature_042 effect is approximately +2.4 months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_011)', df).fit() + per-race t-tests
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 treatment effect by race: white +1.107, hispanic +1.115, black +1.060, asian +1.062, other +1.007. All four interaction terms p>0.6. No evidence of race-based effect modification or unequal benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_089)', df).fit() + per-insurance t-tests
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect by insurance: medicaid +1.081, private +1.091, medicare +1.110, uninsured +1.097. All three interaction terms p>0.68. Treatment benefit is uniform across insurance categories.

Within the n=22,788 feature_111+/feature_015− subgroup: mean pfs_months 4.305 (042=0, n=14,754) vs 6.680 (042=1, n=8,034); diff=+2.376 mo. Confirms predicted-responder benefit ~5× the marginal effect (+1.10) seen unstratified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model that adds the feature_042×feature_111×feature_015 interaction to the leading prognostic features yields substantially higher R^2 than the additive-only model.

feature_067, feature_101, and feature_019 retain monotone gradients with pfs_months across tertiles, supporting them as continuous prognostic biomarkers (positive for feature_019, negative for feature_067 and feature_101).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_042*feature_111*feature_015 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_040 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Combined model R^2=0.849 (vs 0.792 without the three-way interaction). All main and interaction coefficients align with prior findings: 042×111 +2.391, 042×111×015 -2.403, 056 -1.553, 080 +0.176/unit, 063 level2 -2.339, 048 -0.980, 040 -0.471, 067 -0.076/unit, 019 +0.477/unit, 101 -0.019/unit.

feature_019 tertile means: lo 4.43 < mid 4.75 < hi 4.96 (positive gradient). feature_067 tertile means: lo 4.97 > mid 4.75 > hi 4.36 (negative). feature_101 tertile means: lo 4.96 > mid 4.79 > hi 4.32 (negative). All monotone, consistent with linear-effect estimates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_111+/feature_015− predicted-responder subgroup, the feature_042 benefit (~+2.4 mo) is consistent across all three feature_063 stage levels.
feature_111 has no effect on pfs_months among feature_042=0 patients (treatment-naive), confirming feature_111 is a predictive (not prognostic) biomarker for feature_042.

feature_015 has no effect on pfs_months in the feature_042=0 stratum but a strongly negative effect within feature_042=1/feature_111=1, confirming feature_015 is a resistance marker, not a prognostic factor.

analyses.code: for level in feature_063 levels: t-test of feature_042 within feature_111+/feature_015- subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect within 111+/015− by feature_063: level0 +2.383 mo (5.254→7.637, n=8,043), level1 +2.394 mo (4.047→6.441, n=11,408), level2 +2.425 mo (2.851→5.276, n=3,337). Magnitude is essentially identical across stage levels; all p-values < 1e-150.

Within feature_042=0 (n=32,572): feature_111 effect on pfs_months = +0.011 mo (4.303 → 4.313), p=0.678. feature_111 has no main prognostic effect; its apparent univariate signal is entirely mediated by the 042×111 interaction.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind feature_015 within feature_042==0 and within 042==1/111==1
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 effect within feature_042=0 stratum is null. Within feature_042=1/feature_111=1 (n=12,194): pfs_months 6.680 (015=0) vs 4.365 (015=1); diff = -2.315 mo, p<1e-300. Confirms feature_015 acts as a resistance/anti-predictive marker that abolishes feature_042 benefit only in the otherwise-responder stratum.
